jokes.text: First of all, I am rage glowing.
jokes.setup: Character says 'rage sweat' as an insult
jokes.punchline: Leslie corrects it to the more elegant 'rage glowing'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Leslie character moment - taking something crude and making it sound prettier. Great economy and character specificity.

jokes.text: What did the P.P.P.P.P. ever do to you?
jokes.setup: Leslie loves government programs and acronyms
jokes.punchline: The absurdly long acronym for Pawnee Palms Public Putt-Putt
jokes.explanation: Classic Leslie bureaucratic language joke - she would absolutely create this ridiculous acronym and use it seriously.

jokes.text: Schools, police, mini-golf, merry-go-rounds, parades, gazebo repair, roads and bridges, whatever. Pretty gardens, hummingbird feeders.
jokes.setup: Leslie listing essential government services
jokes.punchline: Mini-golf gets equal priority with schools and police, ending with frivolous items
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Leslie's priorities are both noble and ridiculous. The casual 'whatever' after major infrastructure is perfect.

jokes.text: The Hoover Dam is a travesty.
jokes.setup: Ron's anti-government stance taken to extremes
jokes.punchline: Calling one of America's greatest engineering achievements a travesty
jokes.explanation: Perfectly captures Ron's extreme libertarian views. The deadpan delivery of something so absurd is peak Ron.

jokes.text: Do you expect me to make a chair out of transistor chips and small wires?
jokes.setup: Leslie suggests Ron go make furniture instead of cutting budgets
jokes.punchline: Ron takes 'solder a chair' literally and imagines electronic furniture
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist logic from Ron taking Leslie's metaphor literally. Shows his practical mind.

jokes.text: And it'll produce double the amount of electricity. - How? - I'll figure it out. It already has windmills. Who cares?
jokes.setup: Leslie claims mini-golf will beat the Hoover Dam in electricity production
jokes.punchline: She has no plan but mentions windmills dismissively
jokes.explanation: Classic Leslie optimism without logic. The casual 'who cares?' about actual engineering is perfect.

jokes.text: - Yeah, I know. - I feel very ill.
jokes.setup: Leslie compliments how handsome both councilmen look
jokes.punchline: One agrees confidently, the other feels sick from the obvious pandering
jokes.explanation: Good contrast between politicians' reactions to obvious flattery. Economical and well-structured.

jokes.text: Well, the last time I was there, I saw kids of all races getting along and playing. It was terrible. So I'm voting to get rid of it.
jokes.setup: Councilman describes a wholesome scene at the mini-golf course
jokes.punchline: He finds racial harmony 'terrible' and wants to destroy it
jokes.explanation: Dark humor that reveals character through shocking misdirection. The casual delivery makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Well, on the one hand, I love cutting government programs. On the other hand, mini-golf rules. And on the third hand, I hate both you and Ron Swanson.
jokes.setup: Jeremy explains his conflicted position as swing vote
jokes.punchline: Escalates from two hands to a third hand, casually admits hating everyone
jokes.explanation: Good escalation structure and reveals Jeremy's petty motivations. The third hand is a nice absurd touch.

jokes.text: I'm kind of a badass wild card.
jokes.setup: Jeremy has just shown he's wishy-washy and petty
jokes.punchline: He thinks this makes him a 'badass wild card'
jokes.explanation: Good irony - his indecision and pettiness are the opposite of badass. Character delusion comedy.

jokes.text: More like who am I doing? No one. I'm free. What's up?
jokes.setup: Jeremy tries to sound sexually active and mysterious
jokes.punchline: Immediately deflates his own bravado by admitting he's alone
jokes.explanation: Good self-deflating joke structure. Jeremy tries to be cool but immediately reveals he's pathetic.

jokes.text: I am? You drove here. Nah, it's too late, bro. I already ordered the water. I don't know what you want me to do.
jokes.setup: Ben points out the logical flaw in Andy being designated driver
jokes.punchline: Andy's absurd reasoning that ordering water makes it unchangeable
jokes.explanation: Perfect Andy logic - once something arbitrary happens, it becomes immutable law. Great character consistency.

jokes.text: And somehow I just ended up becoming the designated driver. And paying for everyone. And I didn't get to choose the bar. I should be more assertive.
jokes.setup: Ben lists how he got taken advantage of
jokes.punchline: His mild 'I should be more assertive' response to being completely steamrolled
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Ben's pushover nature. The understated response makes it funnier.

jokes.text: I didn't recognize us without me because I'm the only one that matters.
jokes.setup: Andy discovers his band playing without him
jokes.punchline: His egotistical explanation for not recognizing them
jokes.explanation: Perfect Andy narcissism disguised as innocent observation. Great character voice.

jokes.text: Hey, baby, can I borrow your car for a month? I already lent you my car. Do you not know where my car is? Ugh, I'm so sick of the third degree. It's like dating a cop.
jokes.setup: Mona-Lisa asks to borrow Tom's car
jokes.punchline: She already has it and doesn't know where it is, then blames Tom for asking questions
jokes.explanation: Great escalation of Mona-Lisa's chaos. The reversal of who's being unreasonable is perfect.

jokes.text: I'm gassy. Let's make out.
jokes.setup: Normal romantic situation
jokes.punchline: Mona-Lisa announces she's gassy then suggests romance
jokes.explanation: Perfect Mona-Lisa chaos - completely inappropriate timing and oversharing. Maximum economy.

jokes.text: Dating Mona-Lisa is awesome. Except that I live in constant fear for my life.
jokes.setup: Tom says dating Mona-Lisa is awesome
jokes.punchline: Immediately contradicts with fear for his life
jokes.explanation: Clean setup/punchline with good contradiction. Shows Tom's poor decision-making clearly.

jokes.text: Calm down, Ann. You're proving my point.
jokes.setup: Tom says all his girlfriends are crazy, Ann objects
jokes.punchline: Tom uses her objection as evidence she's crazy too
jokes.explanation: Good circular logic joke. Tom's manipulative reasoning is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: She once jumped out of a moving car to buy a Nicki Minaj poster.
jokes.setup: Tom explaining Mona-Lisa is legitimately insane
jokes.punchline: The specific absurd example of dangerous poster shopping
jokes.explanation: Excellent specific absurdist detail. The combination of danger and triviality is perfectly Mona-Lisa.

jokes.text: Ugh, but that's my favorite blankie.
jokes.setup: Ann is a professional adult woman
jokes.punchline: She calls her blanket 'blankie' like a child
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Ann's hidden childishness beneath her professional exterior.

jokes.text: I can be a sort of... Conversational lubricant.
jokes.setup: Chris offers to help with small talk
jokes.punchline: His awkward sexual metaphor for conversation
jokes.explanation: Good cringe humor from Chris's inappropriate metaphor. The pause before it makes it worse.

jokes.text: Yeah, there's probably a less gross way to say it, but sure.
jokes.setup: Chris's inappropriate 'conversational lubricant' comment
jokes.punchline: Leslie's understated acknowledgment of how gross it sounded
jokes.explanation: Perfect reaction to Chris's weirdness. Leslie's diplomatic but honest response is in character.

jokes.text: Way to be, duck.
jokes.setup: Chris giving positive reinforcement
jokes.punchline: He encourages a literal duck with the same energy as people
jokes.explanation: Perfect Chris moment - his relentless positivity extends to wildlife. Great character consistency.

jokes.text: Some fat Hawaiian guy left it in my waiting room. When he came back, I was all like, 'Oh, uh, yeah, yeah, no, I haven't seen it. I don't know.' Classic.
jokes.setup: Jamm compliments his putter
jokes.punchline: He casually admits to stealing it and lying to the owner's face
jokes.explanation: Great Jamm character moment - casual theft and racism delivered as if it's charming. Dark but effective.

jokes.text: You know I have irritable bowel syndrome, you racist.
jokes.setup: Leslie offers hot dogs
jokes.punchline: Jamm calls her racist for not knowing his medical condition
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection - takes innocent food offer and makes it about prejudice. Peak Jamm manipulation.

jokes.text: First of all, your nachos are terrible. No one's had the guts to tell you. Why don't you melt the cheese?
jokes.setup: Andy confronts the band about playing without him
jokes.punchline: Burly immediately attacks Andy's terrible nacho-making skills
jokes.explanation: Great deflection tactic and reveals Andy's incompetence at even basic food prep. Good structure.

jokes.text: Well, I dropped my cell phone in a bowl of cereal last week, you idiot.
jokes.setup: Andy complains they didn't call him
jokes.punchline: His phone is in cereal, calling them idiots for not knowing
jokes.explanation: Perfect Andy logic - destroys his own property then blames others. The cereal detail is wonderfully specific.

jokes.text: Maybe you should change your name to Burly and The Backstabbers. That's a pretty good name, actually.
jokes.setup: Andy delivers what should be a devastating insult
jokes.punchline: He immediately undercuts it by admitting it's actually a good band name
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection. Andy can't even stay mad because he's genuinely impressed with his own insult. Perfect character moment.

jokes.text: You guys sound like school.
jokes.setup: Leslie and Ron arguing about taxpayer costs and civic duty
jokes.punchline: Jamm reduces their serious political debate to sounding like boring school
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jamm dismissal - reduces complex issues to simple annoyance. Great economy and character voice.

jokes.text: For friendship.
jokes.setup: Chris announces they're about to set a course record
jokes.punchline: It's a record 'for friendship' rather than golf
jokes.explanation: Perfect Chris moment - turning competition into emotional bonding. The deadpan delivery sells it.

jokes.text: We can do this, but I will bite you.
jokes.setup: Ann approaches Mona-Lisa for a civil conversation
jokes.punchline: Mona-Lisa agrees but threatens violence immediately
jokes.explanation: Perfect Mona-Lisa chaos - agrees to talk but maintains threat of biting. Great character consistency.

jokes.text: Do you know his ATM pin? 'Cause I cannot crack that little bitch's code.
jokes.setup: Mona-Lisa and Ann having a woman-to-woman talk about Tom
jokes.punchline: She's trying to steal from his bank account and calls him a bitch
jokes.explanation: Dark humor revealing Mona-Lisa's criminal intentions and casual misandry. Shocking but funny.

jokes.text: How did I not know that Diddy was on Instagram, you jagweeds?
jokes.setup: Ann mentions Tom's Instagram coasters from Diddy
jokes.punchline: Mona-Lisa is outraged at missing this celebrity social media development
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Mona-Lisa's priorities and the word 'jagweeds' is perfectly random.

jokes.text: Sometimes when you use a computer, it gets hot, and the fan cools it down.
jokes.setup: April asks why Andy needs a computer fan for songwriting
jokes.punchline: Andy explains computer fans as if she's never heard of them
jokes.explanation: Good Andy moment - completely missing the point and explaining the obvious instead.

jokes.text: Tommy owns his own business. Yeah, but he had to borrow a lot of money, and when you're not around, Tom drinks tap water.
jokes.setup: Mona-Lisa defends Tom's financial status
jokes.punchline: Ann reveals the devastating truth - he drinks tap water when alone
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character detail - tap water as the ultimate sign of poverty for status-conscious people. Perfect escalation.

jokes.text: Mm, yeah, 'cause I don't eff with poorsies.
jokes.setup: Mona-Lisa learning Tom is broke
jokes.punchline: Her casual classism with made-up slur 'poorsies'
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about class discrimination. 'Poorsies' is a perfectly awful Mona-Lisa invention.

jokes.text: I don't even usually like Puerto Rican chicks. I'm not--I mean, it shouldn't matter, but I'm--
jokes.setup: Mona-Lisa breaking up with Tom
jokes.punchline: Her casual racism toward Ann, who tries to correct the assumption
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment showing Mona-Lisa's casual prejudice and Ann's awkward correction attempt.

jokes.text: Math is worthless in real life. I mean, there's an app for calculating tips. That's all you need.
jokes.setup: Snow cone vendor wants to go do calculus homework
jokes.punchline: Jamm's reductive view of mathematics based on tip apps
jokes.explanation: Good Jamm ignorance - reducing all mathematics to restaurant tipping. In character.

jokes.text: You sure make a guy feel like a real lady.
jokes.setup: Jamm enjoying Leslie and Ron competing for his vote
jokes.punchline: His confused gender metaphor about feeling special
jokes.explanation: Good malaprop from Jamm - mixing metaphors about feeling wanted. The confusion is perfect.

jokes.text: Well, after tonight, you'll only know me as the former artist formerly known as the lead singer of Mouse Rat.
jokes.setup: Andy announcing his retirement from music
jokes.punchline: His convoluted riff on Prince's 'artist formerly known as' title
jokes.explanation: Good wordplay attempt from Andy, though the execution is appropriately clunky for his character.

jokes.text: ♪ He made incredible nachos that everybody claimed they loved ♪
jokes.setup: Andy's swan song about his life
jokes.punchline: Including the nacho criticism in his tragic song
jokes.explanation: Andy incorporating the band's hurtful nacho feedback into his dramatic farewell song

jokes.text: She covered me in glitter and Anna Nicole body spray. Why do they still even make that? They don't--she's had the same bottle for years. She just mixes in water and baby oil.
jokes.setup: Tom explaining his disheveled appearance
jokes.punchline: Escalation from weird perfume to Mona-Lisa's DIY perfume creation
jokes.explanation: Great escalation revealing Mona-Lisa's chaos. The homemade perfume detail is perfectly gross.

jokes.text: Why is there a gorilla guarding this gingerbread house?
jokes.setup: Playing mini-golf and encountering course obstacles
jokes.punchline: Ron's logical question about the absurd mini-golf theming
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor about mini-golf absurdity. Ron's practical mind questioning fantasy logic.

jokes.text: Uh, bean sprouts. Tofu. Ralph Nader. Ayn Rand is a terrible writer.
jokes.setup: Leslie trying to distract Ron from his crucial putt
jokes.punchline: Her escalating list of things that would bother a libertarian
jokes.explanation: Good escalation from hippie food to direct ideological attack. Shows Leslie knows Ron's triggers.

jokes.text: What the mother-effing, c-ing ess-ing, effing k-ing eff is going on right now?
jokes.setup: Mona-Lisa finding Tom and Ann together
jokes.punchline: Her elaborate censored swearing that makes no sense
jokes.explanation: Great Mona-Lisa moment - her attempt at TV-safe swearing creates incomprehensible word salad.

jokes.text: You guys want a threesome. What? No. We do not wanna have a threesome with you. Well, let's not rush to judgment. I mean, I say we hear her out.
jokes.setup: Mona-Lisa misreads the situation completely
jokes.punchline: Tom immediately considers her terrible idea despite the circumstances
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Tom's terrible decision-making. Even in crisis, he considers bad options.

jokes.text: Funny goofball music Andy... Is gone. You're left with the professional remains.
jokes.setup: Andy claiming to be a serious adult now
jokes.punchline: His dramatic language while still being obviously childish
jokes.explanation: Good irony - Andy trying to sound mature while using ridiculous language like 'professional remains.'

jokes.text: I will call him '$9,000 of taxpayer money the gorilla.'
jokes.setup: Ron naming the gorilla Leslie bought to shame him
jokes.punchline: His literal, bureaucratic name that emphasizes the waste
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron response - turns Leslie's gesture into a constant reminder of government waste.

jokes.text: That is a terrible-- his name is Mr. Fuzzyface!
jokes.setup: Ron's bureaucratic gorilla name
jokes.punchline: Leslie's childish name and emotional investment in it
jokes.explanation: Good character contrast - Leslie's emotional attachment vs Ron's cold practicality. Shows their fundamental differences.

jokes.text: She then pretends those are ecstasy and sells it to a bunch of college kids that are drinking Goldschläger at a gas station.
jokes.setup: Tom describing Mona-Lisa's crime spree
jokes.punchline: The specific details about fake drugs and gas station drinking
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation with specific absurd details. The Goldschläger at a gas station is perfectly trashy.

jokes.text: My word is garbage. Everyone knows that.
jokes.setup: Jamm admits he was going to break his promise
jokes.punchline: His casual admission that his promises are worthless
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jamm self-awareness - he knows he's untrustworthy and doesn't care. Great economy.

jokes.text: 'Twas Leslie killed the beast.
jokes.setup: Leslie apologizing for blaming Ron for the gorilla's metaphorical death
jokes.punchline: Her dramatic Shakespearean language about mini-golf
jokes.explanation: Good Leslie dramatic flair applied to trivial situation. The archaic language is perfectly over-the-top.

jokes.text: And then you either realize that I'm right or you get hungry and go home and I win.
jokes.setup: Leslie describing ideal political discourse
jokes.punchline: Her casual admission that hunger often determines political outcomes
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor about how trivial factors affect serious debates. Very Leslie perspective.

jokes.text: And there's a certain code of honor that accompanies gift-giving. God, you're the worst.
jokes.setup: Ron explaining why he won't return the gorilla
jokes.punchline: Leslie's frustrated response to his rigid principles
jokes.explanation: Good character dynamic - Ron's principles frustrating Leslie who just wants practical results.

jokes.text: That's right, two lead vocalists. Name one other band that's done that. The Beatles.
jokes.setup: Andy proudly announces his innovative two-vocalist concept
jokes.punchline: April immediately names the most obvious example
jokes.explanation: Excellent setup/punchline structure. Andy's pride immediately deflated by the most obvious counterexample.
